question.text: The rate of time value erosion (time decay) in options is:

answer.text: Non-linear — it accelerates as the expiry date approaches
Linear — equal amount of decay each day
Exponential — faster at the start, slower near expiry
Zero until the last week before expiry

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Time decay is non-linear — the rate of erosion INCREASES as expiry approaches. An option loses more time value in its last month than in the first month. **The Trap:** It's not linear (equal daily). Not exponential in the way described. Decay starts from day one, not just the last week. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers the theta decay curve. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option sellers benefit most from the last 30 days of accelerated decay — selling short-dated options captures this rapid erosion most efficiently.
